feedback.text: I support this Act but would like it to be amended to also consider the problems in regard to digital ownership, specifically videogames. This field is extremely wild-west and has little to no protections for the consumer and would need some kind of workup. Problems that i see that have to be amended : - Consumers need ample protections regarding digital products they have already paid for, which would otherwise lead to the inevitable permanent loss of said product. - Games such as TheCrew have been very noteworthy as these videogames have been fully playable in an offline-state but have been rendered unplayable due to the implementation of online-checks to sell the consumer additional content. This product was rendered inaccessible in its entirety simply because of those online checks. - There are numerous consumers that would get stripped of their purchases simply because of this unregulated market. - Videogames are a form of art, and its a shame and crime to let companies destroy them on a whim forever, for every single consumer simply because of their unwillingness to implement end-of-life plans to give the consumers a way to access their purchase post-end-of-life. This is not calling for endless support of product, merely an end-of-life plan to keep it accessible in an offline-state. Additionally i'd like to call for an extended look at the business practices of Big Players in the Industry. These have sadly reached a point where gambling practices, dark patterns and addictice design of digital services have become a norm, instead of a dark corner of the Internet. Adding to this, some Publishers/Developers still keep the Design Philosophy of making the cancellation process of a subscription a hassle in order to keep people away from actually cancelling their subscription. Thanks for your time.
